article_title: FBI and CISA Warn Russian Intelligence Is Hijacking Signal and WhatsApp Accounts in Global Phishing Campaign

content_preview.title: FBI and CISA Warn Russian Intelligence Is Hijacking Signal and WhatsApp Accounts in Global Phishing Campaign
content_preview.summary: A joint FBI-CISA advisory says Russian-linked actors have compromised thousands of Signal and WhatsApp accounts belonging to government officials, military personnel, and journalists across multiple countries.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The FBI and CISA issued a joint public service announcement on March 20 warning that threat actors affiliated with Russian intelligence services are conducting a large-scale phishing campaign targeting users of Signal, WhatsApp, and other encrypted messaging platforms. The campaign has resulted in the unauthorized compromise of thousands of accounts worldwide, according to the advisory tracked as [PSA 2026/032-0320](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fbi-links-signal-phi...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article with clear Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, and Defensive Recommendations sections. Excellent international context with cascade of warnings. 625 words appropriate for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources verified. BleepingComputer confirmed: PSA tracking, Russian attribution, thousands compromised, target profiles. CyberScoop confirmed: Google TIG quote about growing prevalence. NBC News confirmed: Dutch AIVD/MIVD March 9 warning, 'large-scale global attempt', Dutch govt employees compromised, Germany February warning. Euronews confirmed: verification code theft, Signal support quote verbatim, France C4 alert.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Two attack techniques (QR code device linking + verification code theft) confirmed across multiple sources. International warning timeline (Germany Feb, Dutch March 9, US/France March 20) verified. Signal quote matches Euronews verbatim. Article correctly notes this is social engineering, not encryption bypass.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately attributes claims to specific agencies without editorializing. Good transparency about unknowns.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of this FBI-CISA advisory. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent cybersecurity article with strong multi-source corroboration across 4 reputable outlets. All claims verified. Ready for publication.
